title: Beyond Bidets Lies the Overlooked Plumbing of AI

content:

title: Revealed: How wealthy Brits are using social media for massive investment decisions
description: New research which surveyed Britons earning £100,000 or more about their investment choices suggests many high earners rely on social media and Artificial Intelligence for advice.
content:
Nearly one third of high-earning Brits admit they use social media to make major investment decisions, with video-sharing app TikTok emerging as the most popular platform for financial guidance.

title: We must stop AI reversing progress women have made in workplace
description: Few chief executives are treating inclusion as part of their strategic response to the rise of artificial intelligence, writes the new women in finance champion
content:
Britain can congratulate itself on progress for women in business. Women now hold more than 42 per cent of board seats across FTSE 350 companies — a figure that would have seemed implausible when I began my career in banking.
But scratch the surface ... [3897 chars]

title: Is AI productivity prompting burnout? Study finds new pattern of "AI brain fry"
description: Artificial intelligence can give some workers "brain fry" if overused, according to a new study published in Harvard Business Review.
content: The promise of artificial intelligence has been simple: let the machines do the work. Instead, it may be creating a new headache from babysitting the machines. A new study published in Harvard Business Review suggests that instead of making work easi... [4042 chars]
